paper_title: Material and Methods of Fossil Specimens Study

methods.description: A provenance analysis was carried out in amber samples using Fourier Transform Infrared (FTIR) micro-spectroscopy to determine the origin and composition of the amber.

steps.description: Amber samples were collected by anonymous indigenous miners.
steps.searchable_summary: collect amber samples from mining site

steps.description: A provenance analysis was carried out in amber samples using FTIR micro-spectroscopy.
steps.searchable_summary: perform FTIR micro-spectroscopy on amber samples

methods.description: 3D X-ray micro-computed tomography scanning was used to acquire detailed images of the fossil specimens embedded in amber.

steps.description: X-ray microtomography images were acquired using a benchtop micro-CT built at the Physics Institute (IF), UNAM.
steps.searchable_summary: acquire X-ray microtomography images using benchtop micro-CT

steps.description: The images were corrected for flat-field non-uniformities, dead-pixels, and dark noise.
steps.searchable_summary: correct images for flat-field non-uniformities, dead-pixels, and dark noise

steps.description: The Feldkamp-Davis-Kress algorithm, a Hamming filter with 0.7 cut-off frequency, and an in-house developed program written in MATLAB were applied for tomographic reconstruction.
steps.searchable_summary: apply Feldkamp-Davis-Kress algorithm and Hamming filter for tomographic reconstruction

steps.description: The open source programs ImageJ and OsiriX were used for 3D image post-processing and displaying.
steps.searchable_summary: use ImageJ and OsiriX for 3D image post-processing and display
